annotations.label: Quantum electrodynamics (QED)

anchor.snippet: is the relativistic quantum field theory of electrodynamics

annotations.note: Quantum field theory of electrodynamics is a physics construct with no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Renormalization

anchor.snippet: This procedure was named renormalization

annotations.note: Renormalization of QFT divergences is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Three basic actions of QED

anchor.snippet: The key components of Feynman's presentation of QED are three basic actions

annotations.note: Feynman's three basic QED interactions have no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Probability amplitude

anchor.snippet: for the numerical quantities called probability amplitudes

annotations.note: Quantum probability amplitudes are not a formalized notion in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sum rule for indistinguishable alternatives

anchor.snippet: then its probability amplitude is the sum of the probability amplitudes of the alternatives

annotations.note: Superposition/sum rule for amplitudes is a physics axiom with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Product rule for independent sub-processes

anchor.snippet: the probability amplitude of the total (compound) process is the product of the probability amplitudes of the sub-processes

annotations.note: Multiplication of amplitudes for composed sub-processes is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Compton scattering

anchor.snippet: the name given to this process of a photon interacting with an electron in this way is Compton scattering

annotations.note: Compton scattering is a physical process absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Born rule (square of amplitude length)

anchor.snippet: the probability of an event is the square of the length of the corresponding amplitude arrow

annotations.note: The Born rule relating probability to squared amplitude modulus is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Addition and multiplication of amplitude arrows

anchor.snippet: The product of two arrows is an arrow whose length is the product of the two lengths

annotations.note: Amplitude arrows are complex numbers and their multiplicative-length rule is the verified multiplicativity of norm (`norm_mul`), but the physics framing is not formalized.

annotations.label: Antisymmetry under electron exchange (Fermi–Dirac)

anchor.snippet: the resulting amplitude is the reverse – the negative – of the first

annotations.note: Fermionic antisymmetry of exchange amplitudes is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Feynman propagators

anchor.snippet: These are called Feynman propagators

annotations.note: Feynman propagators have no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Divergences requiring mass renormalization

anchor.snippet: the simple correction mentioned above led to infinite probability amplitudes

annotations.note: Self-energy divergences and mass renormalization are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: QED as abelian gauge theory U(1)

anchor.snippet: QED is an abelian gauge theory with the symmetry group U(1)

annotations.note: The symmetry group U(1) is formalized as the abelian group `Circle`, but the notion of a gauge theory is not.

annotations.label: QED Lagrangian / action

anchor.snippet: gives rise to the action

annotations.note: The QED Lagrangian density and action functional are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dirac matrices

anchor.snippet: are Dirac matrices

annotations.note: A grep found no Dirac/gamma matrices in Mathlib (`Dirac` occurrences refer to the Dirac measure).

annotations.label: Dirac spinor field

anchor.snippet: a Dirac spinor field of spin-1/2 particles

annotations.note: No spinor or spinor-field definitions exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dirac adjoint

anchor.snippet: is sometimes referred to as the Dirac adjoint

annotations.note: The Dirac adjoint of a spinor is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gauge covariant derivative

anchor.snippet: is the gauge covariant derivative

annotations.note: The U(1) gauge covariant derivative is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coupling constant

anchor.snippet: is the coupling constant , equal to the electric charge of the Dirac spinor field

annotations.note: The QED coupling constant (electric charge) is a physical parameter not represented in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Covariant four-potential

anchor.snippet: is the covariant four-potential of the electromagnetic field generated by the electron itself

annotations.note: The electromagnetic four-potential is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Electromagnetic field tensor

anchor.snippet: is the electromagnetic field tensor

annotations.note: The electromagnetic field strength tensor is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Second form of the QED Lagrangian

anchor.snippet: reveals a second useful form of the Lagrangian

annotations.note: Alternative forms of the QED Lagrangian are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conserved Noether current

anchor.snippet: current arising from Noether's theorem

annotations.note: Noether's theorem (symmetry-conservation correspondence) and its conserved current are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equation of motion for ψ (Dirac equation)

anchor.snippet: so the equation of motion can be written

annotations.note: The Dirac equation as an equation of motion is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equation of motion for A μ (wave equation / Maxwell)

anchor.section: Equation of motion for A μ
anchor.snippet: which is a wave equation for the four-potential, the QED version of the classical Maxwell equations in the Lorenz gauge

annotations.note: Maxwell's equations / the four-potential wave equation are not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.section: Equation of motion for A μ
anchors.snippet: which is a wave equation for the four-potential, the QED version of the classical Maxwell equations in the Lorenz gauge

annotations.label: S-matrix

anchor.snippet: This technique is also known as the S-matrix

annotations.note: The scattering S-matrix is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Evolution operator / interaction Hamiltonian

anchor.snippet: where time evolution is given by the interaction Hamiltonian

annotations.note: The interaction-picture evolution operator and interaction Hamiltonian are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dyson series

anchor.snippet: is called the Dyson series

annotations.note: The time-ordered Dyson series is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invariant amplitude

anchor.snippet: it is convenient to define the invariant amplitude

annotations.note: The invariant (matrix) amplitude is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: S-matrix via Wick's theorem and Feynman diagrams

anchor.snippet: all the terms of the S-matrix for quantum electrodynamics can be computed through the technique of Feynman diagrams

annotations.note: Wick's theorem and Feynman-diagram expansion of the S-matrix are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Compton scattering amplitude

anchor.snippet: An example is Compton scattering , with an electron and a photon undergoing elastic scattering

annotations.note: The Compton scattering amplitude computation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Schwinger effect

anchor.snippet: This process, called the Schwinger effect

annotations.note: The Schwinger pair-production effect is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Renormalizable theory

anchor.snippet: the theory is said to be "renormalizable"

annotations.note: The notion of a renormalizable QFT is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Criterion for renormalizability

anchor.snippet: A criterion for the theory being meaningful after renormalization is that the number of diverging diagrams is finite

annotations.note: The power-counting criterion for renormalizability is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dyson's argument: zero radius of convergence

anchor.snippet: the radius of convergence of the perturbation series in QED is zero

annotations.note: Radius of convergence of a power series is formalized (`FormalMultilinearSeries.radius`), but Dyson's physical argument that the QED series has radius zero is not.

annotations.label: Landau pole

anchor.snippet: The coupling constant runs to infinity at finite energy, signalling a Landau pole

annotations.note: The Landau pole / running coupling is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Extension to curved spacetime

anchor.snippet: This theory can be extended, at least as a classical field theory, to curved spacetime

annotations.note: Extension of electrodynamics to curved spacetime is not formalized in Mathlib.
